title: zapoteca caracasana subsp. caracasana
intro: Zapoteca caracasana subsp. caracasana, also known as Zapote de San Luis, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It is a small, deciduous tree or shrub with a range of medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties, which can help alleviate congestion and improve breathing. Traditionally, Zapoteca caracasana subsp. caracasana is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
